summary.intent: Fix a bug where the Whisper STT provider ignored the configured model and language fields, then get the fix reviewed and merged.
summary.outcome: Bug fixed with full test coverage, PR #643 opened against main, and a Greptile inline comment about template wording resolved and pushed.

repo: SttProviderId::Whisper is instantiated in crates/gateway/src/voice.rs; GroqStt is the reference implementation for multi-field STT constructors.
The project uses a with_options pattern for STT constructors that accept api_key, model, and language.
just lint runs clippy+fmt but also triggers a macOS CUDA build for llama-cpp-sys-2, causing spurious failures unrelated to most changes.
Greptile is configured as an automated PR reviewer and leaves inline comments.

code.finding: SttProviderId::Whisper branch previously called WhisperStt::new(Some(k)), hardcoding whisper-1 and dropping the language config field entirely.
WhisperStt had no language field; added language: Option<String>, with_options constructor, and fallback logic in transcribe so self.language is used when request.language is None.
Added commented [voice.stt.whisper] example block for discoverability; wording was later revised in response to a Greptile PR comment.

workflow: cargo check -p <crate1> -p <crate2> is used for fast targeted compile checks rather than a full workspace build.
The project pins a nightly toolchain (nightly-2025-11-30) for fmt and clippy.
Background bash tasks can be launched and their output retrieved later via output files.

friction: just lint blew up on a pre-existing macOS CUDA build issue in llama-cpp-sys-2, making it unreliable as a lint gate for unrelated changes.
WhisperStt lacked a language field entirely, so the fix required both the struct and the call site to be updated — the bug was more pervasive than a single-line fix.
open_items: The llama-cpp-sys-2 macOS CUDA build failure surfaced by just lint was not filed as a bug and should be tracked separately.
PR #643 was created but not yet merged; merge into main is pending review/approval.
